title: NOC 31110 – Dentists | GTR Immigration
description: Dentists remain in strong demand across Canada due to population growth, increased awareness of oral health, and expanding private and public healthcare services. Both general dentists and specialists are actively recruited in urban and regional communities. Classified under NOC 31110 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: Examine teeth, gums, and oral tissues
Perform complex oral and facial surgeries
Treat jaw deformities and facial injuries
Design and fit braces and corrective appliances
Replace missing teeth using implants or dentures
Design and supervise fabrication of dental prostheses
employment_requirements: Degree from a recognized dental program
Successful completion of National Dental Examining Board (NDEB) certification
Orthodontists: 3-year advanced orthodontics program accredited by the Canadian Dental Association
Provincial licensing required in all cases
Without provincial licensure, dentists cannot independently practice in Canada.
job_outlook: This indicates a positive outlook, particularly for:
🦷 Planning Canada PR as a Dentist?

GTR Immigration can evaluate your eligibility under NOC 31110, guide you through credential recognition and provincial licensing, and help structure the most strategic immigration pathway to practice in Canada.
pathways: Opportunities to own or partner in clinics
High demand in suburban and rural areas
additional_information: Private dental clinicsMulti-specialty dental centersPublic health unitsHospitals and surgical centersUniversities and teaching institutions
Typical annual earnings: $30,200–$261,517 CAD, depending on province, specialization, clinic ownership, and patient volume.
Specialists such as orthodontists, oral surgeons, and prosthodontists often earn significantly above the national median.
